findings.title: Open Port Detected (22/SSH)
findings.description: Port 22 running SSH is open on the target host.
findings.evidence:
Service: SSH
Banner: SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_6.6.1p1 Ubuntu-2ubuntu2.13
findings.remediation: Ensure this port is firewalled and not exposed to the public internet unless required. If running, ensure it uses secure credentials and up-to-date software versions.

findings.title: Open Port Detected (80/HTTP)
findings.description: Port 80 running HTTP is open on the target host.
findings.evidence:
Service: HTTP
Banner: None captured
findings.remediation: Ensure this port is firewalled and not exposed to the public internet unless required. If running, ensure it uses secure credentials and up-to-date software versions.

findings.title: Missing HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) Header
findings.description: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) instructs the browser to always connect using HTTPS, preventing SSL stripping attacks.
findings.evidence: Header 'strict-transport-security' was not found in response.
findings.remediation: Add the 'Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains' header to your web server configuration.

findings.title: Missing Content Security Policy (CSP) Header
findings.description: Content Security Policy (CSP) restricts the resources (such as JavaScript, CSS, Images) that the browser is allowed to load for a given page, offering defense-in-depth against XSS.
findings.evidence: Header 'content-security-policy' was not found in response.
findings.remediation: Implement a robust Content Security Policy header. For example: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self';

findings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
findings.description: The X-Content-Type-Options response HTTP header is a marker used by the server to indicate that the MIME types advertised in the Content-Type headers should not be changed and be followed.
findings.evidence: Header 'x-content-type-options' was not found in response.
findings.remediation: Configure the web server to send the X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header.

findings.title: Missing X-Frame-Options Header
findings.description: X-Frame-Options prevents the website from being loaded in an iframe or object, which protects users against clickjacking attacks.
findings.evidence: Header 'x-frame-options' was not found in response.
findings.remediation: Configure the web server to send X-Frame-Options: DENY or X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN header, or use the CSP frame-ancestors directive.

findings.title: Missing Referrer-Policy Header
findings.description: The Referrer-Policy HTTP header controls how much referrer information (sent via the Referer header) should be included with requests.
findings.evidence: Header 'referrer-policy' was not found in response.
findings.remediation: Add the Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin header to web responses.

findings.title: Missing Permissions-Policy Header
findings.description: Permissions-Policy allows developers to selectively enable, disable, and modify the behavior of various APIs and browser features (camera, geolocation, etc.).
findings.evidence: Header 'permissions-policy' was not found in response.
findings.remediation: Add a Permissions-Policy header configured with restricted features (e.g., geolocation=(), camera=()).

findings.title: Missing Cache-Control Header
findings.description: The response does not include a Cache-Control header. Without explicit caching directives, browsers and proxies may cache the response, potentially storing sensitive data on disk or in shared caches.
findings.evidence: Cache-Control header was not found in the response.
findings.remediation: Add 'Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate' to responses containing sensitive or user-specific data.

findings.title: Web Server Signature Disclosure
findings.description: The server header discloses details about the web server backend software and potentially its version.
findings.evidence: Server: Apache/2.4.7 (Ubuntu)
findings.remediation: Disable or strip the 'server' header in your web server configuration (e.g., exposeHeaders / ServerTokens off).

ssl.error: Failed to negotiate SSL connection: [WinError 10061] No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it
